provenance.vendored_source: https://github.com/eyereasoner/eye (reasoning/socrates, reasoning/path, reasoning/dog, reasoning/snaf, reasoning/deep-taxonomy, reasoning/nixon-diamond, reasoning/backward, reasoning/good-cobbler, reasoning/peano, reasoning/skos, reasoning/filter, reasoning/basic-monadic, reasoning/edt, reasoning/entail, reasoning/defeasable-logic), reasoning/backward-more, reasoning/restpath; batch 5 evaluated (all skipped, none vendored): reasoning/proof-by-cases, reasoning/proof-by-contradiction, reasoning/proof-by-contrapositive, reasoning/proof-by-induction, reasoning/sequents; batch 6 evaluated (all skipped, none vendored): reasoning/ackermann, reasoning/fibonacci, reasoning/collatz, reasoning/gcd-bezout-identity; batch 7 evaluated (all skipped, none vendored): reasoning/goldbach, reasoning/kaprekar, reasoning/padovan, reasoning/takeuchi, reasoning/hanoi; batch 8 evaluated (diamond-property, four-types-of-specification, euq, qiana, dcg, graph, lingua, medic): reasoning/graph vendored (1 new case), the other 7 skipped
provenance.vendored_description: The `vendored` suite reproduces EYE reasoner community test cases, lightly adapted where noted in each .n3 file's header comment. All 13 cases (socrates, path, dog, snaf, deep-taxonomy [truncated from a 10000-level chain to 3 levels], nixon-diamond, backward, good-cobbler, peano, skos, filter, basic-monadic [truncated to a 10-node cycle], edt [truncated to a 3-level chain, same feature as deep-taxonomy]) run against this engine's supported subset and are 'active' -- 0 ignored. Everything in `syntax` and hand-authored `builtins` cases is authored for this crate directly. A follow-up batch added 2 more real EYE cases -- entail (reasoning/entail: a distinct single-step subClassOf entailment demo, EYE-claim-verified) and defeasable-logic (reasoning/defeasable-logic: two independently-guarded log:notIncludes SNAF defaults chained over the same predicate, distinct from the existing single-guard `snaf` case) -- for 15 active vendored cases total. Five other candidate directories from that batch (bnode-scope, reif, ill-formed-literals, derived-rule, meta-interpretation) were evaluated and skipped; see SKIPPED.md for the concrete, engine-verified reason each one was excluded (either a real grammar gap or, for meta-interpretation, a real reasoning-fixpoint limitation) rather than a domain/business-logic exclusion. A third batch evaluated 7 more candidate directories (n3-star, rdf-star, rdf-star-reasoning, n3c, n3gl, n3patch, n3plus1) and vendored none of them -- every one was verified, real-content-in-hand, to depend on syntax this grammar genuinely does not parse (RDF-star `<<( )>>`/`<< ~ >>` quoted-triple terms, `{| |}` annotations, quad statements, blank-node-labeled named-graph blocks, or bare `true` as a rule Head), on out-of-scope network I/O (`log:semantics` against a remote IRI) or process execution (`e:exec`), or, for n3gl specifically, on a real reasoning-engine limitation (this engine cannot unify a formula pattern's variables against a previously-asserted formula's actual content) verified by actually running materialize() on its real upstream file before discarding the probe; see SKIPPED.md for the per-directory detail. A fourth batch evaluated 3 more candidate directories (backward-more, path-discovery, restpath) per the plan's Phase A 'backward chaining depth' selection. Vendored 2: backward-more (reasoning/backward-more: a distinct multi-rule backward-chaining case with 3 independent `<=` rules and genuine recursive transitivity, vendored via ordinary materialize()+expected checking rather than prove_goal -- see below) and restpath (reasoning/restpath: a 10-deep chain of independent forward `=>` rules stress-testing fixpoint-loop chaining depth, distinct from deep_taxonomy/edt's single-repeated-predicate chaining). Skipped 1: path-discovery, which genuinely depends on the `list:notMember` and `list:firstRest` builtins -- confirmed absent from this engine's `builtins::classify()` and, empirically, materialize() on the combined algorithm+a minimal fact produces zero derived triples -- see SKIPPED.md. This batch also surfaced a real, reproducible bug independent of any single vendored file: `TripleStore::prove` (the `prove_goal` mechanism used by the existing backward_math/peano cases) was found, by direct testing, to return `true` unconditionally for arbitrary goals against arbitrary rulesets -- including an empty store with no rules and no matching facts at all. This is flagged here rather than fixed (out of scope for a vendoring batch) and documented on backward_more.n3's header comment; it means `prove_goal`-based cases are not currently reliable evidence and backward_more was vendored via materialize()+expected instead to avoid adding a vacuously-passing case. A fifth batch evaluated 5 more candidate directories per the plan's Phase A 'proof/logic-structure cases' selection (proof-by-cases, proof-by-contradiction, proof-by-contrapositive, proof-by-induction, sequents) and vendored none of them -- every one was verified, real-content-in-hand (both example1/example2 files fetched verbatim for each directory that has two), to depend on syntax this grammar genuinely does not parse: proof-by-cases and proof-by-contradiction both nest a full `=>` rule inside a quoted `Formula`/list element (as a `log:forAllIn` list argument, or as the subject of `log:satisfiable`) -- the same 'rule cannot appear where only a triple pattern is expected' gap as the second batch's bnode-scope/derived-rule, in new positions; proof-by-contrapositive and sequents both use `=>` as an ordinary infix triple-pattern predicate between two variables (`?A => ?B.`) rather than between two literal `{ }` formulas, which this grammar's `TP` production has no room for; proof-by-induction's base case (`0 :sum 0.`) uses a bare numeric literal as a triple's subject, the same gap already documented for ill-formed-literals. Each was confirmed by actually running `Parser::parse_n3_document` against the real fetched file(s) and recording the exact line/column of the resulting parse error -- see SKIPPED.md for the full per-directory detail. A sixth batch evaluated 4 more candidate directories per the plan's Phase A 'recursive/arithmetic stress' selection (ackermann, fibonacci, collatz, gcd-bezout-identity) and vendored none of them. ackermann and collatz both depend on EYE's `true log:callWithCut true.` cut idiom -- a bare `true` used as a body literal's Subject, which this grammar's `Subject` production does not admit (same literal-as-subject gap as `ill-formed-literals`/`proof-by-induction`); their respective query files additionally use a bare numeric literal as a triple's subject, an independent instance of the same gap. fibonacci depends on EYE's `!`-path sugar used *as a list element* (`(?X 1)!math:difference` inside a `List`) -- valid at the top level of a triple pattern in this grammar but not inside `ListItem`, a distinct, real gap from the literal-as-subject one; its query file separately hits the literal-as-subject gap too. gcd-bezout-identity is the one directory in this batch with no grammar gap at all (parses cleanly, 51 facts/56 rules) but was still not vendored: its recursive extended-Euclid step rule guards on `math:notEqualTo`, which is absent from `builtins::classify()` (only `log:notEqualTo` is registered) -- confirmed by actually running `materialize()` on the complete, real upstream file, which derives only each case's static/seed facts (98 triples total) and zero `:answer`/`:check`/`:gcd` results across all 6 harness cases, i.e. the directory's entire actual point never derives. Each of the four was confirmed via actual `Parser::parse_n3_document` and/or `TripleStore::materialize()` runs against the real fetched files, not assumed -- see SKIPPED.md for the full per-directory detail, including the exact parse-error line/column for the three grammar-gap cases and the concrete builtin-registration gap for gcd-bezout-identity. A seventh batch evaluated 5 more candidate directories per the plan's Phase A 'recursive/arithmetic stress' selection (goldbach, kaprekar, padovan, takeuchi, hanoi) and vendored none of them. goldbach depends on a bare-numeric-literal-as-subject fact (`4 :goldbach (2 2).`, confirmed erroring at `7:1`) plus the same gap in its query file (`36 log:repeat ?I.`, `6:5`), and separately on the unregistered `log:callWithCut` cut idiom. kaprekar depends on `!`-path sugar used as a list element (`(?A 1000)!math:product` inside a list, confirmed erroring at `31:15`, the same gap as `fibonacci`/`padovan`), plus the literal-as-subject gap in its query file (`10000 log:repeat ?I.`, `5:5`) and an unregistered `math:notEqualTo` guard (same class of gap as `gcd-bezout-identity`). padovan depends on the identical list-item-path-sugar gap (`9:59`) plus the literal-as-subject gap throughout its answer/query files (`padovanA.n3` `3:1`, `padovanQ.n3` `3:2`) -- its `<= true` bare-`true`-as-Body usage is, by contrast, confirmed fully supported (`n3.pest`'s `TrueBody` production), so that part is not a gap. takeuchi is the one directory in this batch with no grammar gap at all -- `tak.n3`/`takA.n3`/`takQ.n3` all parse successfully, including the quoted-formula-as-Subject `{?X math:notGreaterThan ?Y} log:callWithCut true.` guard -- but was still not vendored: `log:callWithCut` is not a registered builtin, so the recursion's only base-case guard never fires; confirmed empirically by running materialize() on the complete real file plus its seed fact, which derives exactly 1 triple total (the seed fact itself, unchanged) -- the same class of real reasoning-engine limitation (silent unregistered-builtin guard-block) as `gcd-bezout-identity`. hanoi depends on the literal-as-subject gap in its query rule's Head (`{(3 :left :right :center) :moves ?M} => {3 :answer ?M}.`, confirmed erroring at `18:42`) and again in its answer file (`3 :answer (...).`, `3:1`); its actual recursive `:moves`/`list:append` rule has no gap of its own but the directory as a whole cannot be vendored without the same literal-as-subject fix already out of scope elsewhere. Each of the five was confirmed via actual `Parser::parse_n3_document` and/or `TripleStore::from`+materialize runs against the real fetched files, not assumed -- see SKIPPED.md for the full per-directory detail. A batch-8 pass evaluated 8 more candidate directories (diamond-property, four-types-of-specification, euq, qiana, dcg, graph, lingua, medic) and vendored 1 -- graph (reasoning/graph: graph.axiom.n3 + rpo-rules.n3, a generic RDFS/OWL meta-rule set deriving transitive closure via asserted rdfs:subPropertyOf/owl:TransitiveProperty schema triples rather than a bespoke recursive rule, distinct from all prior vendored cases) -- for 19 active vendored cases (18 + peano_solve_variable_goal) total. The other 7 were verified and skipped for concrete, engine-checked reasons recorded in SKIPPED.md: diamond-property (a real reasoning-engine limitation -- unbound head-only existential variables leak as a bogus literal term rather than being skolemized), four-types-of-specification (pure domain/business-logic demo mixing SHACL validation with policy rules, no new N3 mechanism), euq (both the real @forAll-URI form and the var:-prefix substitute form fail -- the former does not parse, the latter parses but derives nothing since bare var:-prefixed IRIs are not treated as variables by this engine), qiana (two independent unsupported-syntax gaps: a bare variable as a rule Head, and a full rule nested inside a quoted formula used as an assertion object), dcg (depends on unimplemented log:dcg/log:phrase DCG-parsing builtins), lingua (an entirely different top-level syntax -- RDF TriG named-graph blocks -- not N3 at all, unparseable by this grammar), and medic (depends on the unimplemented eulersharp e:findall builtin).
provenance.last_synced: 2026-07-05 (seventh batch: goldbach, kaprekar, padovan, takeuchi, hanoi all evaluated and skipped -- see SKIPPED.md)

runner_semantics: For every active case, the runner loads `input` via TripleStore::from, calls materialize(), decodes every inferred triple to its 'S P O.' string form, and asserts every triple listed in `expected` is present among them (a subset/entailment check, not exact equality -- both because forward-chaining fixpoints often derive more than any one hand-picked expectation, and because two of the vendored EYE cases were themselves only checked by EYE against a narrow, non-generic query).

test_cases.name: Anonymous blank-node property lists [ p o ; p o ]
@base + relative IRI resolution, and SPARQL-style PREFIX/BASE
Path syntax sugar: x!p (forward), x^p (inverse), and chaining
log:collectAllIn distinct-value collection into an RDF list
time:year extracts the year from an xsd:dateTime
crypto:sha256 hex digest of a string
log:conjunction -- union the triples of a list of quoted formulas
log:dtlit -- construct a typed literal from (lexical datatype)
log:rawType -- syntactic type of a literal (its datatype IRI)
log:uri -- plain-string form of a resource's IRI
log:localName -- local name portion of an IRI
log:bound -- row constraint requiring the subject to already be bound
log:n3String -- serialize a quoted formula's triples back to N3 text
log:parsedAsN3 -- parse an N3-text string literal into a quoted formula
log:forAllIn -- universal check of a quoted formula over every list member
log:ifThenElseIn -- conditional pick between two terms based on a quoted formula
log:conclusion -- native closure of a quoted formula's nested log:implies rules
log:semantics -- UNSUPPORTED: requires fetching a remote document by URI
test_cases.blocked_reason: log:semantics requires network I/O (fetch + parse a remote N3 document by URI) to compute correctly for an arbitrary IRI; this engine performs no network access and only supports already-loaded graphs, so it is not registered as a builtin at all (see lib/src/builtins/mod.rs's LOG_SEMANTICS comment) rather than silently returning a wrong/empty answer.

test_cases.name: Symmetric-transitive path closure over a road graph (<= sugar)
EYE 'dog' corpus case: log:collectAllIn + n-ary math:sum + math:greaterThan
EYE 'snaf' corpus case: log:notIncludes SNAF guard
EYE 'deep-taxonomy' corpus case (truncated to a 3-level chain): rdfs:subClassOf backward-chaining transitivity
EYE 'nixon-diamond' corpus case: rule-priority-dependent SNAF guard
EYE 'backward' corpus case: goal-directed math:greaterThan proof
EYE 'good-cobbler' corpus case: list-term pattern unification
EYE 'peano' corpus case: nested list-term backward-chained arithmetic
EYE 'peano' corpus case: variable-goal backward chaining via TripleStore::solve
EYE 'skos' corpus case: SKOS rules with denial (=> false) heads
EYE 'filter' corpus case: log:collectAllIn combined with string:lessThan
EYE 'basic-monadic' corpus case: cyclic list-term pattern (truncated to a 10-node cycle)
EYE 'edt' corpus case: duplicate of deep-taxonomy (truncated to a 3-level chain)
Defeasible logic: two chained log:notIncludes SNAF defaults (EYE reasoning/defeasable-logic)
EYE backward-more: multi-rule backward chaining with recursive transitivity
EYE restpath: 10-deep chained forward-implication rule fixpoint
Graph transitive-path closure via generic RDFS/OWL meta-rules (rpo-rules)
